description: Fired when a user completes GitHub OAuth and receives a JWT. Distinguishes new signups from returning logins based on whether the user was just created.
Fired when a user creates a new QA project. Tracks the name and whether a GitHub repo was linked at creation time.
Fired when a user deletes a project, including all cascaded children. Useful for churn signals.
Fired when a user connects a GitHub repo to a project and registers a webhook. Key activation event.
Fired when a user disconnects a GitHub repo from a project.
Fired when a user creates a test suite within a project.
Fired when a user adds a new test case to a suite.
Fired when a user starts a new test run against a set of cases. Tracks whether the run is tied to a PR.
Fired when a test run is finalized. Core value-delivery event — includes pass/fail counts and whether a PR status check was posted.
Fired when a user files a bug report, optionally linked to a test run and case. Tracks severity.
Fired when a user syncs a bug report to a GitHub Issue. Key feature-adoption metric.
Fired when GitHub delivers a pull_request webhook and Prova creates a PR-opened notification. Server-side event capturing inbound GitHub activity.
